title: Eyes on arts
bodyText:
GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Georgians can pick and choose their weekend entertainment from a large variety of events.Below is our weekly calendar for the arts: Eyes on arts.These entries are sent to George Herald on a weekly basis.Have a look and book your tickets for some stunning entertainment on your doorstep.


> 12, 17 and 19 October:


Carpe Musicam! Orchestra & Choir presents The Rest of the Best at Laerskool George-Suid (17th, 19:00) and Mossel Bay Town Hall (19th, 15:30).

Tickets cost R150 (adults) and R50 (scholars), available on www.carpemusicam.co.za.


> 17 October 19:00 and 18 October 15:00 and 19:00:


In the Beginning at the George Arts Theatre. A dance production by Motion Art Academy. In the Beginning tells the story about each of the seven days of creation as described in the Bible. It shows the greatness of our Creator and is filled with Scripture and promises to bring hope and a new sense of wonder and gratitude for who we are and how we came into existence.

Tickets cost R150. Bookings through www.georgeartstheatre.com.


> 24 October:


Art installation from 18:00, show at 19:00: The Hovering: A Living Presence at the Market Community Theatre. Singer, composer and artist Petronel Baard and Louisa Punt-Fouché, an acclaimed academic, writer, artist and poet, present The Hovering, an immersive artistic experience that combines a live performance, art installation, music album, and the official launch of Punt-Fouché's latest book, Breathing with the Earth.

Tickets cost R200 and are available through Quicket or Heather Stead on 072 364 3304.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
